title: This Portfolio Site
description: A responsive personal portfolio built from scratch with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, including a dark mode toggle and a few scroll-based animations.
details: Built entirely with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, no framework, no build step. Features a dark/light theme toggle with a circular reveal transition, scroll-triggered reveal animations, a subtle 3D tilt effect on project cards, and skill tags that automatically link to the projects that use them. Skills, certifications, and projects are all data-driven from JSON files so the content is easy to keep up to date.

title: Charity Donation App
description: An iOS donation app with a SwiftUI profile screen built from reusable components, backed by AWS Lambda and EC2 for file storage and backend logic. Built in Xcode using modern Swift practices.
details: Designed and built a responsive SwiftUI user profile interface using reusable, composable views. Backed the app with AWS Lambda functions and EC2 for backend logic, with S3-backed storage for user file uploads. Built in Xcode following modern Swift and SwiftUI conventions in an agile, iterative workflow, with an emphasis on clean state management and reusable UI components.

title: Attendance Tracker
description: An attendance system with a Java REST API behind a React front-end, plus a companion iOS app in Swift that uses barcode scanning and biometric login to check students in.
details: Hosted a Java REST API server behind a React front-end for managing class attendance records. Built a companion iOS app in Swift with barcode scanning for fast check-ins and biometric login (Face ID) to prevent buddy-punching. Attendance data is stored in MySQL and served through the same API consumed by both the web dashboard and the iOS app.

title: Skin Cancer Detection Model
description: A convolutional neural network trained on 10,000+ images across 7 classes of skin lesions, reaching 89% classification accuracy after tuning the model.
details: Trained a convolutional neural network on a dataset of 10,000+ dermoscopic images spanning 7 classes of skin lesions. Used NumPy and Pandas for preprocessing and Keras/TensorFlow for model training, iterating on architecture and hyperparameters to reach 89% classification accuracy on the held-out test set.
